etymology.description: semantic 鳥(鳥) (niǎo, "bird") + phonetic 凡(凡) (fán) -> mythical bird
etymology.interpretation: The character 鳳 combines 鳥(鳥) (niǎo, "bird"), indicating its connection to the avian world, with 凡(凡) (fán) as a phonetic component, hinting at its pronunciation. This construction symbolizes the mythical phoenix, often associated with auspicious omens and revered in various cultural myths. Its use draws from the elegant and rare nature attributed to this legendary bird.
etymology.simplification: 凤(鳳): The component 鳥(鳥) (niǎo, "bird") was replaced with 又(又) (yòu, "again"), simplifying the strokes necessary to write the character.

examples.chinese: 凤凰涅槃重生(鳳凰涅槃重生)。
examples.english: The phoenix rises from the ashes to be reborn.

examples.chinese: 这是传说中的凤凰(這是傳說中的鳳凰)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì chuánshuō zhōng de fènghuáng.

examples.chinese: 凤舞九天(鳳舞九天)，象征着自由和力量(象徵著自由和力量)。
examples.pinyin: Fèng wǔ jiǔ tiān, xiàngzhēng zhe zìyóu hé lìliàng.
examples.english: The phoenix dances in the sky, symbolizing freedom and strength.
